title: Cabinet Door Monitoring
dataSources: Bring **Smart cabinet lock events** into Splunk via the integration path documented for **Cabinet lock sensor input**, landing on **`index=physical`** as **`sourcetype="cabinet_lock"`** with parsed fields **`rack_id`**, **`user`**, **`action`**, **`authorized`**, and **`method`** aligned to the catalog SPL contract. Typical patterns are **HTTP Event Collector** posts to **`/services/collector/event`**, **Splunk Connect for Syslog** ([Splunkbase app **4740**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4740)) on a Heavy Forwarder, or **REST API Modular Input** ([Splunkbase **1747**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1747)) when the gateway only exposes HTTPS exports; store tokens in `storage/passwords` (or your vault) rather than dashboard tokens.
spl:
index=physical sourcetype="cabinet_lock"
| where action="opened" AND NOT authorized="true"
| table _time, rack_id, user, action, method
description: Flags smart cabinet lock telemetry when a door reports `action=opened` without a matching `authorized=true` (or equivalent) flag, pointing to possible piggyback entry, defeated latch, forced open, or mis-configured credential handoff for a specific rack enclosure.
value: Late awareness of enclosure-level access weakens custody of high-value gear, lengthens forensic timelines after hardware swaps or cable taps, and complicates attestations where regulated or customer workloads expect every physical touch to reconcile to an approved identity and change record.
implementation: Normalize lock events so `authorized` is boolean-consistent, ensure `_time` reflects the controller’s event time, forward only the physical index, schedule the catalog search every five minutes with suppression on `rack_id`, and wire alert actions to physical security with an explicit drill to confirm `action=opened` + `authorized!=true` matches the controller’s live view.
visualization: Sortable Table of `_time`, `rack_id`, `user`, `action`, `authorized`, and `method` for triage; Timeline of open/close pairs per enclosure; Bar chart of unauthorized-style opens by `rack_id`; Single value KPI counting distinct affected racks in the period; optional drilldown to correlated **Access control system** searches when `user` is present.
cimSpl:
| tstats `summariesonly` count
  from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication
  by Authentication.user, Authentication.action, Authentication.src span=1h
| sort - count
dataModelAcceleration: Keep the primary unauthorized-open detection on raw `index=physical` `sourcetype="cabinet_lock"`; enable **Authentication** data model acceleration only after `FIELDALIAS`/`EVAL` maps lock `user` and `action` into the CIM Authentication fields and a volume review shows the model build cost is justified for reporting slices.

references.title: REST API Modular Input on Splunkbase (1747)
Splunk Documentation — Send HTTP Event Collector data to Splunk Enterprise
Splunk Documentation — Create and manage alerts

knownFalsePositives: (1) **`authorized` write delay on `maintenance_bridge` host** — During controller failovers the lock may log `opened` milliseconds before the access-control bridge sets `authorized=true`; suppress duplicate `rack_id` events within a two-second `dedup` window or require `controller_role=active` in the feed. (2) **`overnight_patch_window` firmware reloads** — Technicians legitimately open enclosures while badging is bypassed at the lock; mute when ServiceNow change tags or `work_order` fields are present for that `rack_id` interval. (3) **`badge_replay_queue` backlog** — Stale credential sync can leave `authorized=false` even for valid staff; correlate with **Access control system** grants on the same `user` before paging, or throttle alerts when sync lag metrics exceed baseline. (4) **`syslog_archive_replay` after disk recovery** — Forwarders may resend historical `opened` rows; dedupe on vendor event IDs or `_cd` before alert evaluation so replay traffic does not simulate an incident.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Splunk Enterprise or Splunk Cloud search heads plus an ingestion tier approved for facility physical security; follow `docs/implementation-guide.md` for `inputs.conf` / HEC placement and certificate trust.
• Install or approve the collectors that implement **Cabinet lock sensor input** for your locks: prefer HEC `POST /services/collector/event` from the lock gateway, **Splunk Connect for Syslog** (Splunkbase **4740**), or **REST API Modular Input** (Splunkbase **1747**) with staggered cron (for example every one to five minutes) and secrets in `storage/passwords`.
• RBAC: grant a role such as **`physical_security_analyst`** search access to **`index=physical`** only; use a scheduler service account with `schedule_search` and `edit_alert_actions` while keeping API keys out of Simple XML tokens.
• Field QA: verify `rack_id`, `user`, `action`, `authorized`, and `method` appear in Interactive Search over `-15m` before promoting the alert — mismatch here is the leading cause of silent false negatives.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
• Map each physical cabinet to stable `rack_id` values shared with CMDB labels; reject feeds that omit `authorized` when the vendor can supply it.
• If syslog is unstructured, add `props.conf` `REPORT`/`EXTRACT` rules so `authorized` normalizes to true/false strings the `where` clause can evaluate.
• For API pollers, document the HTTPS path (for example vendor **/events** exports) and rate limits beside the Splunk modular input stanza.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Save the catalog SPL, set `cron_schedule=*/5 * * * *`, `dispatch.earliest_time=-5m`, `dispatch.latest_time=now`, and `alert.suppress` keyed on `rack_id` for at least two intervals to limit duplicate pages during sustained maintenance.

```spl
index=physical sourcetype="cabinet_lock"
| where action="opened" AND NOT authorized="true"
| table _time, rack_id, user, action, method
```

Understanding this SPL
• Base filter uses **`index=physical`** and **`sourcetype="cabinet_lock"`** — keep these synchronized with **Data sources** above when you rename indexes in multi-tenant stacks.
• `where action="opened" AND NOT authorized="true"` is the unauthorized-open heuristic; extend with `maintenance_window` lookups if your feed tags them.
• `table` projects columns analysts need for ticketing, badging correlation, and CCTV time sync.

Step 3 — Validate
Replay a supervised open with a known-good badge and confirm Splunk shows `authorized=true` rows that do **not** trip the alert; then trigger a deliberate policy test (controller-forced open in a lab cabinet) and confirm firing rows match the lock head-end alarm printout.

Step 4 — Operationalize and troubleshoot
Publish a dashboard with the results table plus a timechart of opens by `rack_id`; if volume spikes, check HEC ACK failures, syslog backlogs on the Heavy Forwarder, or clock skew greater than sixty seconds between the lock controller and Splunk indexer time.
grandmaExplanation: We watch the physical data center for power, cooling, environment, and physical access.

controlTest.positiveScenario: During an approved red-team drill on rack `R-14B`, the **Cabinet lock sensor input** stream records `action=opened`, `authorized=false`, and `method=override` while the operator presents no matching credential handoff; the scheduled saved search fires within five minutes, returns `_time`, `rack_id=R-14B`, and `user=drill_operator`, and the Splunk job SID matches the controller’s exported `.csv` for the same second boundaries.
controlTest.negativeScenario: For sixty minutes of routine traffic, every `sourcetype=cabinet_lock` row is either `action=closed` or `opened` with `authorized=true`; the detection stays empty even though **index=physical** counts rise normally.

evidence: Retain the Splunk alert job or saved-report output listing `_time`, `rack_id`, `user`, `action`, and `method`, capture the matching lock-controller or **Cabinet lock sensor input** gateway log excerpt for the same UTC window, and file the physical security analyst sign-off tying the event to badge or CCTV review.
exclusions: Does not certify mechanical lock integrity, replace on-site guard procedures, or prove chain-of-custody for assets never instrumented with smart locks; logical identity governance, visitor management, and non-Splunk physical access tools stay out of scope unless their signals are already normalized into this feed.
equipmentModels: Smart cabinet lock gateway forwarding syslog or JSON to Splunk
Per-rack electronic lock module reporting door latch and authorization state
